description: Feature ID: JURr0uzE The new feature dialog has 2 buttons.  Currently Hold and Save.   Change hold to "Save to Backlog" and "Save".  Add a new button called "Submit to Assistant". Pressing the Submit to Assistant should submit the text in the Title and Description to the server.  The server should use the prompt from the prompts/feature_assist.md file.  That file contains replacement markers {featureName} and {details}.   Replace those in the prompt with the title (featureName) and description text (details).   Use the coding agent with that prompt and return the result back to the TITLE and DESCRIPTION based on the output from the prompt.
planPoints: 1. Update dialog HTML: rename "Hold" to "Save to Backlog", add "Submit to Assistant" button — updated templates.ts dialog footer

2. Add server endpoint `POST /api/features/assist` that reads `feature_assist.md`, replaces `{featureName}` and `{details}`, runs via KaiClient, and returns parsed title/description — added route + parseFeatureAssistResponse in routes.ts
3. Add client-side `submitToAssistant()` function that calls the new endpoint and updates the title/description fields — added in client.js
4. Wire up the new button click handler in the event delegation section — added click handler for nf-assist in client.js
5. Test the build compiles and lint passes — typecheck clean, lint clean (only pre-existing warnings)
title: Submit Feature to AI for Refinement
summary: The feature adds a "Submit to Assistant" button to the new feature dialog that sends the title and description to a Claude agent for refinement via a new `/api/features/assist` endpoint. The server loads a prompt template with placeholders for the feature name and details, runs it through the agent, and returns the improved text. The client-side handler updates the dialog fields with the AI-enhanced response and displays any clarification questions from the assistant.

Now step 3 — add the client-side `submitToAssistant()` function. I'll add it right after `submitNewFeature`:
Now step 4 — wire up the click handler. I also need to update the `submitNewFeature` function to reference the new assist button for disabling:

Now step 5 — verify the build compiles and lint passes:

npm run typecheck 2>&1
npm run lint 2>&1

TypeScript compiles clean and lint only has pre-existing warnings (no errors). Let me update the feature file:The feature is now complete. Here's what was implemented:

1. **Dialog buttons** — Renamed "Hold" → "Save to Backlog" and added a new "Submit to Assistant" button in the New Feature dialog footer.

2. **Server endpoint `POST /api/features/assist`** — Loads the `prompts/feature_assist.md` template, substitutes `{featureName}` and `{details}` with the user's title and description, runs it through the Claude agent via `KaiClient.run()`, and parses the structured response (FEATURE TITLE, DESCRIPTION, optional CLARIFY sections) back as JSON.

3. **Client-side `submitToAssistant()`** — Calls the new endpoint, then populates the title input and Ace editor description with the AI-refined content. Shows a "Working…" indicator on the button during processing and displays any clarification questions from the AI in the error area.

4. **Button state management** — All three buttons (Save to Backlog, Submit to Assistant, Save) are properly disabled during any submission to prevent double-clicks.
✅ Feature complete — Cost: $1.2301  Turns: 39  Time: 244.9s
